perturbation.original_text: A. AGI hereby grants a non-exclusive license (the "License") to the Company and its operating subsidiaries now or hereafter existing to use the Good Sam name and logo, including trademarks, trade names, or service marks as designated by AGI (the "Trademarks"), in connection with the sale of such new and used vehicles by the Company that have satisfied such criteria and standards as are established from time to time by AGI (the "Approved Use"). AGI warrants that it has the right and ability to grant the License to the Company to use the Trademarks for the Approved Use in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and, provided that the Company uses the Trademarks for the Approved Use in accordance with the terms of this Agreement, such grant will not infringe upon the rights of any other party.
perturbation.changed_text: A. AGI hereby grants a non-exclusive license (the "License") to the Company and its operating subsidiaries now or hereafter existing to use the Good Sam name and logo, including trademarks, trade names, or service marks as designated by AGI (the "Trademarks"), in connection with the sale of such new and used vehicles by the Company. AGI warrants that it has the right and ability to grant the License to the Company to use the Trademarks for the Approved Use in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and, provided that the Company uses the Trademarks for the Approved Use in accordance with the terms of this Agreement, such grant will not infringe upon the rights of any other party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the condition that vehicles must satisfy certain criteria and standards established by AGI for the 'Approved Use', while still referring to 'Approved Use' later in the same paragraph, creates an internal contradiction. The definition of 'Approved Use' now lacks clarity on the specific conditions vehicles must meet, making it ambiguous how the Company can ensure its use of the trademarks is 'Approved'. This omission introduces uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. AGI hereby grants a non-exclusive license (the "License") to the Company and its operating subsidiaries now or hereafter existing to use the Good Sam name and logo, including trademarks, trade names, or service marks as designated by AGI (the "Trademarks"), in connection with the sale of such new and used vehicles by the Company. AGI warrants that it has the right and ability to grant the License to the Company to use the Trademarks for the Approved Use in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and, provided that the Company uses the Trademarks for the Approved Use in accordance with the terms of this Agreement, such grant will not infringe upon the rights of any other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement that vehicles meet specific criteria ('such criteria and standards as are established from time to time by AGI') for the 'Approved Use' of the trademarks. However, it still references 'Approved Use' later in the same paragraph. This omission contradicts the need for defined standards, making it unclear what constitutes 'Approved Use' and creates a direct conflict regarding the conditions for using the trademarks, as the contradicted text assumes such criteria exist, while the changed text eliminates the explicit statement about them.

perturbation.original_text: As consideration for the License, the Company shall pay to AGI such fees as may be established from time to time by mutual agreement of the Company and AGI. 5. Marketing of Products. AGI and the Company agree to develop and implement a program for marketing certain products and services offered by AGI through the Good Sam program, including, without limitation, the emergency road service program and the extended warranty program offered through Good Sam.
perturbation.changed_text: As consideration for the License, the Company shall pay to AGI such fees as may be established from time to time by mutual agreement of the Company and AGI. 5. Marketing of Products. AGI and the Company agree to develop and implement a program for marketing certain products and services offered by AGI through the Good Sam program, including, without limitation, the emergency road service program.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and the extended warranty program offered through Good Sam' from the list of products and services to be marketed, but leaving the reference to 'Fulfillment Deposits' for warranty products in the subsequent sentence referring to a marketing agreement, creates an internal contradiction. This makes it unclear whether the extended warranty program is included in the marketing agreement, and the 'Fulfillment Deposits' clause becomes ambiguous and unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Marketing of Products. AGI and the Company agree to develop and implement a program for marketing certain products and services offered by AGI through the Good Sam program, including, without limitation, the emergency road service program. Such marketing program will be on terms acceptable to AGI and the Company and shall be subject to standards and procedures designated by AGI. The terms of the marketing program will be set forth in a marketing agreement between AGI and the Company. The marketing agreement will provide, among other things, the terms pursuant to which the Company shall make "Fulfillment Deposits" for warranty products sold as a part of the marketing program.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'and the extended warranty program offered through Good Sam' from the marketing program description while the contradicted text refers to 'Fulfillment Deposits' for warranty products sold as a part of the marketing program. This omission directly contradicts the subsequent reference to 'Fulfillment Deposits' for warranty products, creating an inconsistency regarding whether the warranty program is included in the marketing agreement, making that clause ambiguous and creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of a breach of this Agreement by the Company, the Endorsement may be withdrawn by AGI in its sole discretion and this Agreement terminated pursuant to the terms of Section 3B. In the event of such withdrawal, the Company agrees forthwith to take such action as AGI may request to publicly evidence that the Endorsement has been withdrawn and the Company agrees not to hold the Business out as having the Endorsement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of a breach of this Agreement by the Company, the Endorsement may be withdrawn by AGI in its sole discretion and this Agreement terminated pursuant to the terms of Section 3B. In the event of such withdrawal, the Company agrees forthwith to take such action as AGI may request to publicly evidence that the Endorsement has been withdrawn.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'and the Company agrees not to hold the Business out as having the Endorsement', creates an internal contradiction, because the agreement still requires the Company to publicly acknowledge the withdrawal. But the company is not binded anymore, to not hold the business out as having the Endorsement. If the company still continues to hold the Business out as having the Endorsement this phrase lacks clarity and the purpose to hold the company accountable in this situation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event of a breach of this Agreement by the Company, the Endorsement may be withdrawn by AGI in its sole discretion and this Agreement terminated pursuant to the terms of Section 3B. In the event of such withdrawal, the Company agrees forthwith to take such action as AGI may request to publicly evidence that the Endorsement has been withdrawn.
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'and the Company agrees not to hold the Business out as having the Endorsement' from the original text directly contradicts the implied obligation within the contradicted text. While the company is still required to publicly announce the withdrawal, the modified text removes the explicit prohibition against continuing to falsely represent that the endorsement is still valid. This creates a contradiction because the initial agreement sought to prevent the company from benefiting from a withdrawn endorsement, which the change now allows, creating an ambiguity and a potential breach of contract.
